connections.gloss: bewray (self), that are bidden, call (for, forth, self, upon), cry (unto), (be) famous, guest, invite, mention, (give) name, preach, (make) proclaim(-ation), pronounce, publish, read, renowned, say.

connections.def: akin to the base of 2753; to "call" (properly, aloud, but used in a variety of applications, directly or otherwise):--bid, call (forth), (whose, whose sur-)name (was (called)). see GREEK for
middle voice from 1909 and 2564; to entitle; by implication, to invoke (for aid, worship, testimony, decision, etc.):--appeal (unto), call (on, upon), surname. see GREEK for 1909 see GREEK for

connections.gloss: (call, be) bless(-ed, happy), go, guide, lead, relieve.

connections.def: from 5456; to emit a sound (animal, human or instrumental); by implication, to address in words or by name, also in imitation:--call (for), crow, cry. see GREEK for

connections.gloss: [idiom] burn (incense), [idiom] earnestly, be male, (make) mention (of), be mindful, recount, record(-er), remember, make to be remembered, bring (call, come, keep, put) to (in) remembrance, [idiom] still, think on, [idiom] well.
admonish, charge, earnestly, lift up, protest, call (take) to record, relieve, rob, solemnly, stand upright, testify, give warning, (bear, call to, give, take to) witness.

connections.def: from 3326 and 2564; to call elsewhere, i.e. summon:--call (for, hither). see GREEK for 3326 see GREEK for
from 3326 and 3992; to send from elsewhere, i.e. (middle voice) to summon or invite:--call (send) foreign see GREEK for 3326 see GREEK for
from 2839; to make (or consider) profane (ceremonially):--call common, defile, pollute, unclean. see GREEK for
